steps.title: Identify the key claim in the previous sentence
steps.content: The previous sentence introduces this idea: Lily can be “keenly sensitive to a scene which was the fitting background of her own sensations.” It establishes that Lily is capable of deeply connecting landscapes to her internal emotional state.

steps.title: Match the illustration to the choice
steps.content: The underlined sentence demonstrates this dynamic: the landscape becomes “an enlargement of her present mood,” and Lily discovers “something of herself” in its qualities. This is the concrete illustration of her capability—exactly what the previous sentence introduced. The other choices either confuse which sentence performs which function, misread the emotional tone, or reverse the relationship between sentences. That matches choice D — D is correct.
